channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Maine Democrats say Platner's campaign is trying to influence replacement process
item.description: The state Democratic Party accused Platner's campaign of trying to "put their thumb on the scale" of the process to replace him if he drops his bid for Senate. Platner's team has denied doing so.
item.content:encoded:
The state Democratic Party accused Platner's campaign of trying to "put their thumb on the scale" of the process to replace him if he drops his bid for Senate. Platner's team has denied doing so.

(Image credit: Laura Brett)
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: Trump says he believes the Iran ceasefire is 'over.' And, IOC lifts Russia's suspension
item.description: Trump said that he believes the U.S.-Iran ceasefire is over, but hasn't ruled out talks. And, the International Olympic Committee paves the way for Russian athletes to compete in the 2028 games.
item.content:encoded:
Trump said that he believes the U.S.-Iran ceasefire is over, but hasn't ruled out talks. And, the International Olympic Committee paves the way for Russian athletes to compete in the 2028 games.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: A spirit of resilience helps Venezuelans face healthcare challenges after the quakes
item.description: The economic collapse of Venezuela has pushed its healthcare system to the brink. The disaster is now adding to the stress.
item.content:encoded:
The economic collapse of Venezuela has pushed its healthcare system to the brink. The disaster is now adding to the stress.

(Image credit: Family photo)
item.dc:creator: Durrie Bouscaren

item.title: Trump administration abruptly cancels grants for teen pregnancy prevention
item.description: Health departments, universities and nonprofit grantees had spent months adapting to President Trump's executive orders. They say the funding cut was completely unexpected.
item.content:encoded:
Health departments, universities and nonprofit grantees had spent months adapting to President Trump's executive orders. They say the funding cut was completely unexpected.

(Image credit: Hózhǫ́ Horizons)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: What's behind the push to make peptide therapies more readily available
item.description: Marketed for longevity and wellness benefits, the unproven therapies are sold through a grey market online. Compounding pharmacies say they should be able to make them legally to meet demand.
item.content:encoded:
Marketed for longevity and wellness benefits, the unproven therapies are sold through a grey market online. Compounding pharmacies say they should be able to make them legally to meet demand.

(Image credit: George Frey/Bloomberg)
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: At NATO summit in Turkey, Trump says he believes ceasefire with Iran is 'over'
item.description: President Trump said he believes the current ceasefire with Iran is over following an exchange of attacks between the U.S. and Iran in the latest escalation straining the agreement to end the war.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said he believes the current ceasefire with Iran is over following an exchange of attacks between the U.S. and Iran in the latest escalation straining the agreement to end the war.

(Image credit: Abdullah Guclu)
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: This factory was severely short on workers. Then it offered flexible work
item.description: A GE Appliances plant in rural northwest Georgia was short hundreds of workers amid COVID-19. A flexible work option where some workers can sign up for shifts through an app has eased the pain.
item.content:encoded:
A GE Appliances plant in rural northwest Georgia was short hundreds of workers amid COVID-19. A flexible work option where some workers can sign up for shifts through an app has eased the pain.

(Image credit: Julie Holder for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump says ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is over, pressure mounts for Graham Platner to drop out of Maine Senate race, IOC opens door for Russian athletes to compete in 2028 Games.
item.content:encoded: Trump says ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is over, pressure mounts for Graham Platner to drop out of Maine Senate race, IOC opens door for Russian athletes to compete in 2028 Games.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Le Pen says she'll run for French presidency next year despite court-ordered monitor
item.description: Far-right leader Marine Le Pen says she'll run for the French presidency next year despite being sentenced Tuesday to wear a court-ordered electronic monitor for embezzlement.
item.content:encoded:
Far-right leader Marine Le Pen says she'll run for the French presidency next year despite being sentenced Tuesday to wear a court-ordered electronic monitor for embezzlement.

(Image credit: Aurelien Morissard)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Tehran targets Bahrain and Kuwait after U.S. strikes
item.description: The regional crossfire raised the risks that an interim agreement to halt fighting in the war could break down, putting the Middle East again at risk of a wider conflict.
item.content:encoded:
The regional crossfire raised the risks that an interim agreement to halt fighting in the war could break down, putting the Middle East again at risk of a wider conflict.

(Image credit: Hadi Mizban)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
